topic: Theme 4: The Occupation and the Resistance
subject: french

items.why: Students confuse Pétain (collaborationist Vichy leader) with de Gaulle, who led la France libre from London.

items.correct: De Gaulle's radio appeal from London to keep fighting
items.why: Students misattribute it; the 18 June appeal launched Free France and the call to resist.

items.q: In what year was France liberated?
items.why: Students confuse 1940 (the defeat and start of Occupation) with 1944 (Liberation, including the freeing of Paris).

items.q: From where did General de Gaulle broadcast his appeal of 18 June 1940?
items.why: Correct answer: London. “Vichy” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: What was 'la Résistance' during the Occupation?
items.correct: Networks that fought the German occupation
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Networks that fought the German occupation. “The Vichy police force” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
